description: You are a Network Reliability Engineer troubleshooting intermittent packet drops on an EC2 instance. The drops correlate with micro-bursts of traffic. Because network traffic follows a power-law distribution, a standard linear graph squashes the baseline into an unreadable flat line, making it hard to see if the baseline was creeping up before the burst. Create and save a Graphite dashboard named 'Network Burst Profiling' containing three graphs: 'Linear Baseline' using metric servers.ec2_instance_1.network.bytes_in; 'Logarithmic Intake' using metric logarithm(servers.ec2_instance_1.network.bytes_in, 10); and 'Transient Burst Isolation' using metric removeBelowValue(diffSeries(servers.ec2_instance_1.network.bytes_in, movingMedian(servers.ec2_instance_1.network.bytes_in, 15)), 0). Dashboard name and graph titles must match exactly (case-sensitive).
